growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: corkrey.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

sec.method: reported specific productivity; final titer 8.72 g/L

yields.Ylactate_glucose: 0.89 g lactate/g glucose (~90% of theoretical max)

growth_records.cite: Dooley et al. Expanded genome and proteome reallocation in a novel, robust Bacillus coagulans strain capable of utilizing pentose and hexose sugars. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00952-24. [PMC11575207]
growth_records.snippet: When supplied with 10 g/L glucose, B-768 grew at a rate of 0.59 ± 0.01 h−1 ... glucose uptake rate of 1.67 ± 0.15 g glucose/OD600/h ... lactate ... productivity of 1.46 ± 0.03 g lactate/OD600/h, and a yield of 0.89 ± 0.01 g lactate/g glucos
growth_records.notes: mu=0.59±0.01 h-1 reported. Doubling time computed td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.59=1.175 h. Glucose specific uptake 1.67±0.15 g/OD600/h and lactate specific productivity 1.46±0.03 g/OD600/h both reported (in-text, Fig 5). Yield 0.89±0.01 g lactate/g glucose reported. Units kept as g/OD600/h because no OD600-to-gDW conversion factor is stated (Methods only say DCW is linearly correlated with OD600), so mmol/gDW/h cannot be derived without fabricating a factor.

up.method: reported specific uptake rate (Fig 5); only 3.52±0.45 g/L of ~10 g/L consumed after ~24 h

sec.method: reported specific productivity; final titer 1.57 g/L

growth_records.cite: Dooley et al. Expanded genome and proteome reallocation in a novel, robust Bacillus coagulans strain capable of utilizing pentose and hexose sugars. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00952-24. [PMC11575207]
growth_records.snippet: when growing on xylose, B-768 exhibited a much slower growth rate of 0.32 ± 0.01 h−1 ... consumed 3.52 ± 0.45 g/L out of ~10 g/L xylose ... xylose uptake rate of 0.35 ± 0.04 g xylose/OD600/h ... lactate ... productivity of 0.07 ± 0.01 g lac
growth_records.notes: mu=0.32±0.01 h-1 reported. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.32=2.166 h. Xylose specific uptake 0.35±0.04 g/OD600/h and lactate specific productivity 0.07±0.01 g/OD600/h reported (Fig 5). Yield 0.18±0.02 g lactate/g xylose reported. Units left as g/OD600/h (no OD600-to-gDW factor given). Same-cell-biomass endpoint (3.45 OD600) but overflow lactate metabolism diminished on xylose.

up.method: 80 g/L glucose supplied as sole carbon source; time-resolved consumption in Fig 1D only, no numeric specific rate in text

sec.method: reported final titer in pH-controlled fermentation; not a specific rate

growth_records.cite: Dooley et al. Expanded genome and proteome reallocation in a novel, robust Bacillus coagulans strain capable of utilizing pentose and hexose sugars. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00952-24. [PMC11575207]
growth_records.method: reported (no numeric mu given; growth kinetics only in Fig 1D)
growth_records.snippet: a pH-controlled fermentation was performed with 80 g/L glucose as the sole carbon source ... when the pH was maintained at 6.5, B-768 produced up to 60 g/L lactate at 92.7% of the theoretical maximum yield (Fig. 1D).
growth_records.notes: pH-controlled bioreactor, 80 g/L glucose, pH 6.5, 50°C. Lactate final titer 60 g/L at 92.7% of theoretical maximum yield (both reported). No numeric specific growth rate or specific uptake/secretion rate given in text (only Fig 1D kinetic curves), so mu/td and specific rates left null; lactate captured as final titer, glucose as feed concentration. No derived rates computed to avoid invention.

growth_records.cite: Dooley et al. Expanded genome and proteome reallocation in a novel, robust Bacillus coagulans strain capable of utilizing pentose and hexose sugars. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00952-24. [PMC11575207]
growth_records.snippet: Optimum cell growth was observed between 45°C and 53°C with a maximum growth rate of 0.4 h−1 on glucose and 0.2 h−1 on xylose or cellobiose.
growth_records.notes: Maximum specific growth rate on glucose = 0.4 h-1 (microplate Bioscreen, optimal temperature 45-53°C) reported. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.4=1.733 h. Distinct from the 0.59 h-1 bottle-culture value (different cultivation format). Glucose concentration for the microplate assay not explicitly stated (basal medium composition otherwise fully given); amount left null.

growth_records.cite: Dooley et al. Expanded genome and proteome reallocation in a novel, robust Bacillus coagulans strain capable of utilizing pentose and hexose sugars. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00952-24. [PMC11575207]
growth_records.snippet: Optimum cell growth was observed between 45°C and 53°C with a maximum growth rate of 0.4 h−1 on glucose and 0.2 h−1 on xylose or cellobiose.
growth_records.notes: Maximum specific growth rate on xylose = 0.2 h-1 (microplate Bioscreen, optimal temperature 45-53°C) reported. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.2=3.466 h. Xylose concentration for microplate assay not stated; amount left null.

growth_records.cite: Dooley et al. Expanded genome and proteome reallocation in a novel, robust Bacillus coagulans strain capable of utilizing pentose and hexose sugars. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00952-24. [PMC11575207]
growth_records.snippet: Optimum cell growth was observed between 45°C and 53°C with a maximum growth rate of 0.4 h−1 on glucose and 0.2 h−1 on xylose or cellobiose.
growth_records.notes: Maximum specific growth rate on cellobiose = 0.2 h-1 (microplate Bioscreen, optimal temperature 45-53°C) reported. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.2=3.466 h. Cellobiose concentration for microplate assay not stated; amount left null.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 137618 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/137618

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 137618 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/137618

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
